turns.user: Các nghiên cứu mới nhất về AI
turns.assistant_text:
Dưới đây là một số nghiên cứu và bài viết mới nhất về trí tuệ nhân tạo (AI):

1. **[Emerging Risks to Watch: AI, Data Centers, and Autonomous Vehicles - Insurance Journal](https://www.insurancejournal.com/magazines/mag-features/2026/05/04/868016.htm)**  
   Bài viết này xem xét cách việc sử dụng AI đang thay đổi cảnh quan rủi ro cho ngành bảo hiểm và nền kinh tế rộng lớn hơn. Nó đề cập đến các mối quan tâm về an ninh mạng và quyền riêng tư dữ liệu, cũng như vai trò ngày càng tăng của AI trong các hệ thống tự động.

2. **[AI Marketing Features - Trend Hunter](https://www.trendhunter.com/amp/trends/ADWEEK-House-Possible-Group)**  
   Bài viết này thảo luận về các công cụ AI trong marketing, nhấn mạnh cách mà các hệ thống AI có thể tự động hóa quy trình làm việc và hỗ trợ các nhiệm vụ sáng tạo trong marketing, giúp đội ngũ tập trung vào chiến lược và sáng tạo hơn.

3. **[Agentic AI: Navigating The Evolving Frontier - Forbes](https://www.forbes.com/sites/chuckbrooks/2026/05/08/agentic-ai-navigating-the-evolving-frontier/)**  
   Bài viết này khám phá khái niệm "Agentic AI", nơi mà các hệ thống AI có thể thực hiện các nhiệm vụ độc lập với ít sự trợ giúp từ con người. Nó nhấn mạnh sự chuyển mình từ tự động hóa thụ động sang tự động hóa chủ động.

4. **[GenAI: Moving to the forefront of claims management - InsuranceNewsNet](https://insurancenewsnet.com/innarticle/genai-moving-to-the-forefront-of-claims-management)**  
   Bài viết này xem xét cách AI đang được sử dụng trong quản lý yêu cầu bồi thường, từ việc xem xét tài liệu đến hỗ trợ chiến lược tố tụng, và những thách thức mà các nhà quản lý rủi ro phải đối mặt khi triển khai AI.

5. **[Acrisure: How AI is the Critical Core Engine for Growth - FinTech Magazine](https://fintechmagazine.com/news/acrisure-how-ai-is-the-critical-core-engine-for-growth)**  
   Bài phỏng vấn với Benjamin Funk, Giám đốc Công nghệ & AI của Acrisure, giải thích cách mà AI đang trở thành một phần cốt lõi trong chiến lược phát triển của công ty, không chỉ là một công cụ mà còn là một động lực chính cho sự thành công.

Nếu bạn cần thêm thông tin chi tiết về bất kỳ bài viết nào, hãy cho tôi biết!

items.title: Emerging Risks to Watch: AI, Data Centers, and Autonomous Vehicles - Insurance Journal
items.summary: # Emerging Risks to Watch: AI, Data Centers, and Autonomous Vehicles. We examine how the expanding use of AI is reshaping the risk landscape for insurance–not only within the industry but across the broader economy and physical world. From cybersecurity and data privacy concerns to the infrastructure required to support AI, to its growing role in autonomous systems, understanding where AI-related risks may emerge is essential to managing them effectively. The wide-ranging–and growing–list of AI capabilities and applications makes it difficult to determine how the transformative technology may impact potential losses across specific insurance lines of business. A recently reported security incident involving data access from a popular enterprise generative AI tool highlights how the mass adoption of large language models (LLMs) and the concentration of the office software market may increase the potential for systemic liability exposure across cyber and professional and management liability lines.

items.title: AI Marketing Features - Trend Hunter
items.summary: Adweek published a report on agentic AI tools in marketing, featuring insights from the ADWEEK House Possible Group Chat co-hosted with Mutinex and participants from Hershey, Breeze Airways and PubMatic. The discussion focused on agentic AI systems designed to automate workflows, support measurement and connect large datasets with creative and strategic marketing tasks while maintaining human oversight. Speakers shared examples ranging from media-mix modelling that reduced data preparation time from months to hours to AI-assisted campaign workflows that remove repetitive tasks and allow teams to focus on higher-level planning and creativity. For marketers, agentic AI is increasingly positioned as a productivity layer that frees teams to spend more time on strategy, taste and creative judgment rather than manual execution. 3. **AI-assisted Creative Collaboration** — Tools that blend generative models with human oversight to iterate on strategy, tone and creative assets present opportunities to reshape agency-client workflows and redefine creative production timelines.

items.title: Agentic AI: Navigating The Evolving Frontier - Forbes
items.summary: # Agentic AI: Navigating The Evolving Frontier. As 2026 advances, the digital landscape has evolved to be increasingly proactive and “always-on.” Using technology to guide decisions is leading to the delegation of authority. AI systems that function with machine agency are referred to as "agentic AI." These systems can read intent, plan multi-step activities, use tools, access systems, and carry out tasks independently with little assistance from humans. The age of Agentic AI is transformative rather than incremental. Agentic AI is distinct from passive automation. This moment is characterized by operational autonomy and technical innovation. Agentic AI is increasingly establishing itself as the standard decision-making framework in critical systems. The implication is profound: algorithmic velocity is evolving into a form of national power. Agentic AI expedites the move to autonomous risk. Organizations must transition from reactive cybersecurity to "Security by Design" to mitigate these assaults. In terms of national security, AI autonomy will provide countries with considerable benefits in defense, intelligence, and cybersecurity.

items.title: GenAI: Moving to the forefront of claims management - InsuranceNewsNet
items.summary: # GenAI: Moving to the forefront of claims management - Insurance News | InsuranceNewsNet. HomeNow reading Property and Casualty News. Property and Casualty News RSSGet our newsletter. Claims teams use AI-assisted tools to sort incoming materials, summarize large files and support litigation strategy, while defense counsel uses GenAI to draft outlines, organize chronologies and prepare initial drafts. But for risk managers overseeing claims operations, the question is no longer whether AI should be used, but how to deploy it without introducing new operational, legal or reputational risks. Let’s explore how AI is being used across claims handling and defense, where it delivers tangible value, where it still falls short, and what recent cases and regulatory developments mean for companies managing risk. When it comes to handling and defending claims, AI is already proving most useful in three core areas: document review, case summarization and litigation support.

items.title: Acrisure: How AI is the Critical Core Engine for Growth - FinTech Magazine
items.summary: # Acrisure: How AI is the Critical Core Engine for Growth. Benjamin Funk, Chief Technology & AI Officer at Acrisure, explains how the company’s implementation of AI at its core is key to its success. For the modern business, AI is more than just a means to an end. Aside from the front-end benefits focused on efficiency, AI is adopting a vital role in every boardroom: strategy. Simply put, AI is more than a tool in fintech. It is rapidly becoming the solution to every company’s growth strategy. In this Q&A with FinTech Magazine, Benjamin Funk, Chief Technology & AI Officer at Acrisure, explains how Acrisure’s AI strategy is the core of its operation. At Acrisure, I’m now leading technology, innovation, and AI across our engineering, data, analytics, product, sales tech and agentic automation teams. Not as an overlay, but as a critical and core engine for how a business ought to run.

turns.user: Bạn có thể giúp tôi làm những gì?
turns.assistant_text:
Tôi có thể hỗ trợ bạn trong các lĩnh vực nghiên cứu, tìm kiếm thông tin, và cung cấp nội dung liên quan đến:

1. **Tin tức và sự kiện hiện tại**: Tìm kiếm thông tin mới nhất về các chủ đề cụ thể.
2. **Bài viết và nghiên cứu học thuật**: Tìm kiếm và cung cấp thông tin về các bài nghiên cứu, tài liệu học thuật.
3. **Bài viết trên mạng xã hội**: Tìm kiếm các bài viết hoặc tweet liên quan đến một chủ đề cụ thể.
4. **Đánh giá nguồn thông tin**: Kiểm tra độ tin cậy của một nguồn thông tin hoặc trang web.
5. **Chính sách nội bộ**: Cung cấp thông tin về các chính sách nội bộ của công ty (nếu có).

Nếu bạn có yêu cầu cụ thể nào trong các lĩnh vực trên, hãy cho tôi biết!

turns.user: Tin tức chiến sự ở Iran
turns.assistant_text:
Dưới đây là những tin tức mới nhất về tình hình chiến sự ở Iran:

1. **[Iran freezing exchange of messages with US over Israeli attacks in Lebanon – Tasnim - The Times of Israel](https://www.timesofisrael.com/iran-freezing-exchange-of-messages-with-us-over-israeli-attacks-in-lebanon-tasnim/)**  
   Iran đã ngừng trao đổi thông điệp với Hoa Kỳ thông qua các trung gian do các cuộc tấn công của Israel ở Lebanon. Iran và các đồng minh của mình đã đặt ra kế hoạch để hoàn toàn phong tỏa Eo biển Hormuz và kích hoạt các mặt trận khác để "trừng phạt" Israel và các đồng minh của nó.

2. **[Conflicting U.S. positions, Israeli attacks on Lebanon delay diplomacy - Haaretz](https://www.haaretz.com/middle-east-news/iran/2026-06-01/ty-article/conflicting-u-s-positions-israeli-attacks-on-lebanon-delay-diplomacy/0000019e-82fb-da5c-a9bf-dfffc25e0000)**  
   Iran đã chỉ trích các vị trí mâu thuẫn của Hoa Kỳ và các cuộc tấn công của Israel ở Lebanon là nguyên nhân làm chậm tiến trình ngoại giao. Lực lượng Vệ binh Cách mạng Iran đã tuyên bố rằng họ đã nhắm mục tiêu vào một căn cứ không quân được sử dụng trong các cuộc tấn công của Hoa Kỳ.

3. **[High-Wire Negotiations with Iran? Trump Finds it ‘Very Boring.’ - The New York Times](https://www.nytimes.com/2026/06/01/us/politics/trump-iran-negotiations-boring.html)**  
   Tổng thống Trump đã bày tỏ sự không quan tâm đến các cuộc đàm phán với Iran, cho rằng chúng rất nhàm chán. Ông đã tham gia vào cuộc xung đột Iran trong ba tháng qua, nhưng hiện tại có vẻ như ông không quan tâm đến việc tiếp tục các cuộc đàm phán.

4. **[US trades strikes with divided Iran regime as peace talks stall - Fox News](https://www.foxnews.com/live-news/us-iran-strikes-trump-hormuz-israel-june-1)**  
   Iran đã ngừng các cuộc đàm phán gián tiếp với các nhà đàm phán Hoa Kỳ để phản đối các cuộc tấn công của Israel vào Hezbollah ở Lebanon. Bộ Ngoại giao Iran đã nhấn mạnh rằng Lebanon cần phải được bao gồm trong lệnh ngừng bắn hiện tại giữa Hoa Kỳ và Iran.

5. **[Iran warns Israeli attacks in Lebanon threaten ceasefire with US - BBC](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c202rxp1z15o)**  
   Iran đã cảnh báo rằng các cuộc tấn công của Israel vào Hezbollah ở Lebanon có thể đe dọa lệnh ngừng bắn với Hoa Kỳ. Nếu Israel không ngừng các cuộc tấn công, khả năng lệnh ngừng bắn với Hoa Kỳ sẽ kết thúc là rất cao.

Nếu bạn cần thêm thông tin chi tiết về bất kỳ bài viết nào, hãy cho tôi biết!

items.title: Iran freezing exchange of messages with US over Israeli attacks in Lebanon – Tasnim - The Times of Israel
items.summary: Iran’s Tasnim news agency said on Monday that Tehran’s negotiating team is stopping the exchange of messages with the United States through mediators due to Israel’s attacks in Lebanon, as diplomatic efforts to end the three-month-old Iran war continued. The agency said Iran and the Resistance Front, which includes its Shiite allies in Yemen, Lebanon and Iraq, have set an agenda to completely block the Strait of Hormuz and activate other fronts, including the Bab El Mandeb Strait, to “punish” Israel and its supporters. “The ceasefire between Iran and the US is unequivocally a ceasefire on all fronts, including in Lebanon. “The immediate cessation of the Zionist regime’s aggressive and brutal army operations in Gaza and Lebanon and the necessity of the regime’s complete withdrawal from the occupied areas in Lebanon have been emphasized by Iranian officials and negotiators, and there will be no talks until Iran and the resistance’s views on this matter are met,” Tasnim added.

items.title: Conflicting U.S. positions, Israeli attacks on Lebanon delay diplomacy - Haaretz
items.summary: # Iran Says Contradictory U.S. Positions, Israeli Attacks on Lebanon Delaying Diplomacy. Iran's Revolutionary Guards said their aerospace force had targeted an air base used in a U.S. attack on southern Iran, but did not identify the location of the base. Iran on Monday blamed the slow pace of efforts to end the U.S.-Iran conflict on a lack of trust, contradictory positions from Washington ‌and continued Israeli attacks in the region. ### Click the alert icon to follow topics:. ## In the News. ### Conflicting U.S. Positions, Israeli Attacks on Lebanon Delay Diplomacy. ### Groundhog's Day in Lebanon: Waking Up in 1982. ### Why the Bank of Israel Isn't Fighting the Dollar's Sharp Decline: Trump. ### Pentagon Leaks Signal U.S. Effort to Blame Israel for Risks of Renewed Iran War. Subscribe to join the conversation. ### Who Built the Biblical-era Fortresses in the Deserts of Israel and Jordan? ### Pentagon Leaks Signal U.S. Effort to Blame Israel for Risks of Renewed Iran War.

items.title: High-Wire Negotiations with Iran? Trump Finds it ‘Very Boring.’ - The New York Times
items.summary: # Trump Finds High-Wire Iran Negotiations ‘Very Boring’ - The New York Times. Skip to contentSkip to site indexSearch & Section Navigation Section Navigation. *   U.S. Strikes on Iran. # High-Wire Negotiations with Iran? President Trump told CNBC that he “couldn’t care less” if the negotiations with Iran break down. President Trump at the White House in Washington in May.Credit...Haiyun Jiang/The New York Times. For three months, President Trump has been deeply engaged in the Iran conflict, planning the 38 days of attack, struggling to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and proclaiming “a whole civilization will die tonight,” then backing away to declare a cease-fire and a naval blockage of Iranian ports. “I don’t care if they’re over, honestly,’’ he told Eamon Javers of CNBC when asked about reports that the Iranians, angry at continued Israeli strikes in Lebanon and low-level conflict with the United States in the Persian Gulf, were threatening to stop negotiating. *   ©2026 The New York Times Company.

items.title: US trades strikes with divided Iran regime as peace talks stall - Fox News
items.summary: Iran is halting its indirect talks with U.S. negotiators in protest against Israel's attacks on Hezbollah in Lebanon, Iranian state-linked media reported Monday. The report comes after Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi posted to social media saying Lebanon must be included in the ongoing ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran. The aggressive maneuvering came hours after Trump pitched an expansion of the Abraham Accords, as an analyst said Tehran is seeking to position itself as the region’s "new sheriff" while forcing Gulf states with backchannels to Iran to choose between Washington’s security umbrella and a "New Islamic Civilization.". "The Ministry of Foreign Affairs reiterates the State of Kuwait's condemnation and denunciation, in the strongest terms, of the heinous and repeated Iranian attacks, which represent a dangerous escalation and a direct assault on the security and stability of the State of Kuwait, as well as a flagrant violation of the rules of international law, the United Nations Charter, and Security Council Resolution 2817 of 2026, not to mention the grave threat they pose to the safety of civilians and vital facilities in the country," Kuwaiti officials said in a statement.

items.title: Iran warns Israeli attacks in Lebanon threaten ceasefire with US - BBC
items.summary: Iran has warned that Israeli attacks against Hezbollah in Lebanon could threaten Tehran's ceasefire with the US, after Israel's prime minister ordered strikes on the southern suburbs of Beirut. Earlier Benjamin Netanyahu said "terror targets" in Dahieh would be struck in response to Hezbollah rocket and drone attacks on Israeli civilians and other violations of a ceasefire announced in April that has failed to end the fighting. The news agency - which is affiliated with Iran's powerful Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) - also said Iran and its allies would "activate other fronts, including the Bab al-Mandab Strait" at the entrance of the Red Sea. The Iranian authorities have not publicly commented on the report from Tasnim - but state TV said the probability of the ceasefire with the US ending was high if Israel did not end its offensive in Lebanon. The Israeli military has struck Beirut twice since the ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon came into force on 16 April, most recently on Thursday.

Dưới đây là những tin tức mới nhất về tình hình chiến sự ở Iran:

1. **[Iran freezing exchange of messages with US over Israeli attacks in Lebanon – Tasnim - The Times of Israel](https://www.timesofisrael.com/iran-freezing-exchange-of-messages-with-us-over-israeli-attacks-in-lebanon-tasnim/)**  
   Iran đã ngừng trao đổi thông điệp với Hoa Kỳ thông qua các trung gian do các cuộc tấn công của Israel ở Lebanon. Iran và các đồng minh của mình đã đặt ra kế hoạch để hoàn toàn phong tỏa Eo biển Hormuz và kích hoạt các mặt trận khác để "trừng phạt" Israel và các đồng minh của nó.

2. **[Conflicting U.S. positions, Israeli attacks on Lebanon delay diplomacy - Haaretz](https://www.haaretz.com/middle-east-news/iran/2026-06-01/ty-article/conflicting-u-s-positions-israeli-attacks-on-lebanon-delay-diplomacy/0000019e-82fb-da5c-a9bf-dfffc25e0000)**  
   Iran đã chỉ trích các vị trí mâu thuẫn của Hoa Kỳ và các cuộc tấn công của Israel ở Lebanon là nguyên nhân làm chậm tiến trình ngoại giao. Lực lượng Vệ binh Cách mạng Iran đã tuyên bố rằng họ đã nhắm mục tiêu vào một căn cứ không quân được sử dụng trong các cuộc tấn công của Hoa Kỳ.

3. **[High-Wire Negotiations with Iran? Trump Finds it ‘Very Boring.’ - The New York Times](https://www.nytimes.com/2026/06/01/us/politics/trump-iran-negotiations-boring.html)**  
   Tổng thống Trump đã bày tỏ sự không quan tâm đến các cuộc đàm phán với Iran, cho rằng chúng rất nhàm chán. Ông đã tham gia vào cuộc xung đột Iran trong ba tháng qua, nhưng hiện tại có vẻ như ông không quan tâm đến việc tiếp tục các cuộc đàm phán.

4. **[US trades strikes with divided Iran regime as peace talks stall - Fox News](https://www.foxnews.com/live-news/us-iran-strikes-trump-hormuz-israel-june-1)**  
   Iran đã ngừng các cuộc đàm phán gián tiếp với các nhà đàm phán Hoa Kỳ để phản đối các cuộc tấn công của Israel vào Hezbollah ở Lebanon. Bộ Ngoại giao Iran đã nhấn mạnh rằng Lebanon cần phải được bao gồm trong lệnh ngừng bắn hiện tại giữa Hoa Kỳ và Iran.

5. **[Iran warns Israeli attacks in Lebanon threaten ceasefire with US - BBC](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c202rxp1z15o)**  
   Iran đã cảnh báo rằng các cuộc tấn công của Israel vào Hezbollah ở Lebanon có thể đe dọa lệnh ngừng bắn với Hoa Kỳ. Nếu Israel không ngừng các cuộc tấn công, khả năng lệnh ngừng bắn với Hoa Kỳ sẽ kết thúc là rất cao.

Nếu bạn cần thêm thông tin chi tiết về bất kỳ bài viết nào, hãy cho tôi biết!

items.title: Iran freezing exchange of messages with US over Israeli attacks in Lebanon – Tasnim - The Times of Israel
items.summary: Iran’s Tasnim news agency said on Monday that Tehran’s negotiating team is stopping the exchange of messages with the United States through mediators due to Israel’s attacks in Lebanon, as diplomatic efforts to end the three-month-old Iran war continued. The agency said Iran and the Resistance Front, which includes its Shiite allies in Yemen, Lebanon and Iraq, have set an agenda to completely block the Strait of Hormuz and activate other fronts, including the Bab El Mandeb Strait, to “punish” Israel and its supporters. “The ceasefire between Iran and the US is unequivocally a ceasefire on all fronts, including in Lebanon. “The immediate cessation of the Zionist regime’s aggressive and brutal army operations in Gaza and Lebanon and the necessity of the regime’s complete withdrawal from the occupied areas in Lebanon have been emphasized by Iranian officials and negotiators, and there will be no talks until Iran and the resistance’s views on this matter are met,” Tasnim added.

items.title: Conflicting U.S. positions, Israeli attacks on Lebanon delay diplomacy - Haaretz
items.summary: # Iran Says Contradictory U.S. Positions, Israeli Attacks on Lebanon Delaying Diplomacy. Iran's Revolutionary Guards said their aerospace force had targeted an air base used in a U.S. attack on southern Iran, but did not identify the location of the base. Iran on Monday blamed the slow pace of efforts to end the U.S.-Iran conflict on a lack of trust, contradictory positions from Washington ‌and continued Israeli attacks in the region. ### Click the alert icon to follow topics:. ## In the News. ### Conflicting U.S. Positions, Israeli Attacks on Lebanon Delay Diplomacy. ### Groundhog's Day in Lebanon: Waking Up in 1982. ### Why the Bank of Israel Isn't Fighting the Dollar's Sharp Decline: Trump. ### Pentagon Leaks Signal U.S. Effort to Blame Israel for Risks of Renewed Iran War. Subscribe to join the conversation. ### Who Built the Biblical-era Fortresses in the Deserts of Israel and Jordan? ### Pentagon Leaks Signal U.S. Effort to Blame Israel for Risks of Renewed Iran War.

items.title: High-Wire Negotiations with Iran? Trump Finds it ‘Very Boring.’ - The New York Times
items.summary: # Trump Finds High-Wire Iran Negotiations ‘Very Boring’ - The New York Times. Skip to contentSkip to site indexSearch & Section Navigation Section Navigation. *   U.S. Strikes on Iran. # High-Wire Negotiations with Iran? President Trump told CNBC that he “couldn’t care less” if the negotiations with Iran break down. President Trump at the White House in Washington in May.Credit...Haiyun Jiang/The New York Times. For three months, President Trump has been deeply engaged in the Iran conflict, planning the 38 days of attack, struggling to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and proclaiming “a whole civilization will die tonight,” then backing away to declare a cease-fire and a naval blockage of Iranian ports. “I don’t care if they’re over, honestly,’’ he told Eamon Javers of CNBC when asked about reports that the Iranians, angry at continued Israeli strikes in Lebanon and low-level conflict with the United States in the Persian Gulf, were threatening to stop negotiating. *   ©2026 The New York Times Company.

items.title: US trades strikes with divided Iran regime as peace talks stall - Fox News
items.summary: Iran is halting its indirect talks with U.S. negotiators in protest against Israel's attacks on Hezbollah in Lebanon, Iranian state-linked media reported Monday. The report comes after Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi posted to social media saying Lebanon must be included in the ongoing ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran. The aggressive maneuvering came hours after Trump pitched an expansion of the Abraham Accords, as an analyst said Tehran is seeking to position itself as the region’s "new sheriff" while forcing Gulf states with backchannels to Iran to choose between Washington’s security umbrella and a "New Islamic Civilization.". "The Ministry of Foreign Affairs reiterates the State of Kuwait's condemnation and denunciation, in the strongest terms, of the heinous and repeated Iranian attacks, which represent a dangerous escalation and a direct assault on the security and stability of the State of Kuwait, as well as a flagrant violation of the rules of international law, the United Nations Charter, and Security Council Resolution 2817 of 2026, not to mention the grave threat they pose to the safety of civilians and vital facilities in the country," Kuwaiti officials said in a statement.

items.title: Iran warns Israeli attacks in Lebanon threaten ceasefire with US - BBC
items.summary: Iran has warned that Israeli attacks against Hezbollah in Lebanon could threaten Tehran's ceasefire with the US, after Israel's prime minister ordered strikes on the southern suburbs of Beirut. Earlier Benjamin Netanyahu said "terror targets" in Dahieh would be struck in response to Hezbollah rocket and drone attacks on Israeli civilians and other violations of a ceasefire announced in April that has failed to end the fighting. The news agency - which is affiliated with Iran's powerful Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) - also said Iran and its allies would "activate other fronts, including the Bab al-Mandab Strait" at the entrance of the Red Sea. The Iranian authorities have not publicly commented on the report from Tasnim - but state TV said the probability of the ceasefire with the US ending was high if Israel did not end its offensive in Lebanon. The Israeli military has struck Beirut twice since the ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon came into force on 16 April, most recently on Thursday.
